lessons.title: Defining Functions
lessons.concept_intro: A function is a reusable block of code with a name. You define it once, call it whenever you need it.
lessons.analogy: Like a recipe: write it once in the cookbook, follow it every time you want to make that dish.

lessons.title: Parameters & Return
lessons.concept_intro: Parameters are inputs to a function. Return is the output. You put ingredients in, you get a dish out.
lessons.analogy: A vending machine: put in coins (parameters), press a button, get a snack (return value).

lessons.title: Default Parameters
lessons.concept_intro: Default parameters have a fallback value if none is provided.
lessons.analogy: Like ordering coffee: 'medium' is the default size unless you say otherwise.

lessons.title: Scope
lessons.concept_intro: Variables created inside a function only exist inside that function. This is called 'local scope.'
lessons.analogy: What happens in the function stays in the function — like a private conversation in a room.

lessons.title: Functions Calling Functions
lessons.concept_intro: Functions can call other functions. This is how you build complex programs from simple pieces.
lessons.analogy: Like an assembly line: one worker cuts, another paints, another packages — each does one job and passes the result along.
